content: Remote terminal over WebSocket uses three-tier architecture: (1) Frontend — xterm.js terminal emulator in the browser renders ANSI escape codes, captures keystrokes, sends over WebSocket. (2) Relay/bridge — WebSocket connection carries raw terminal bytes. For cloud relay, the relay server must proxy the PTY stream: daemon-side WS carries PTY output upstream; cloud-side WS carries keystrokes downstream. (3) Daemon backend — node-pty (or Python pty module) spawns a shell in a pseudo-terminal, which handles interactive signals (Ctrl+C, SIGWINCH resize), pipes stdout/stderr upstream. Window resize messages (cols/rows) must be sent as separate control messages and applied to ptyProcess.resize().
